titleStmt.title: SII 11.45: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 45. (B.K. No. 231 of 1928-29). KURHAṬṬI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF HANUMĀN.

summary.p: This inscription, which is much damaged, belongs to the reign of king Āhavamalla (Nūrmaḍi-Taila II) and is dated in the year Vikrama which corresponded to Śaka 902 (= A.D. 980). It refers to a family of local chiefs, descended from Rēvanta, among whom are mentioned: (1) Keṁcha, who acquired by conquest (the) Beḷvala (country) wherein lay Navilgundapura (the modern Navalgund); (2) his eldest son Siri-Mudda; (3) in their family Piriya [Kō]ṭi-gāvuṇḍa who built a Śiva temple and a [Jai]na-śālā; (4) his son Piriya Āycha; (5) in his family Toṇḍa; and (6) his eldest son Kāḷidhurandhara who pleased (king) Kannaradēva (probably the Rāshṭrakūṭa king Kṛishṇa III), by his valour. It mentions also the Vedic scholars Chaturvēdi-Bhaṭṭa, Jātavēda-Bhaṭṭa, Chāmuṇḍa-Bhaṭṭa, also called Guṇanidhi, born in the race of the Ṛishi Agastya, and his younger brother Eṟeyamma-Dīkshita, who was versed in the Lākuḷa-siddhānta, was a śākta and performed many sacrifices. The record registers the gift of the village Kāraṇaguripaṭṭi as an agrahāra to Guṇanidhi and of some money and land made by the king and his subordinate Śōbhana who was ruling over the Beḷvala-Purikara country. Śōbhana is identical with Mahāsāmanta Śōbhanarasa (Dyn. Kan. Dists. p. 432 and Ep. Ind. Vol. IV—p. 205). Kāraṇaguripaṭṭi is the modern Kurhaṭṭi, from which the inscription comes.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© śrīmadravijasphuradurudhāmaṁ rēvanta nāta nanvayado ḷsaṁgrāmaja
yi kencaneṁbō niḷāmahitaṁ vīra narkkasutani nudāraṁ . 1 piḍidaḍasi navila
kondaḍigiḍe taḷtikki bēḍavaḍeyaṁ ballāḷpaḍedaṁ beḷvalamaṁ nōḷvaḍe
munnaṁ baḷikkenele navilgundapuraṁ . 2 mērttale yenisi doṁ kali to
®tuḷiduḷidikke pareya bhānūdayadoḷkarttaleyantire ripugaḷkartta
le vesarādu dātanokkalvesaruṁ . 3 sirimudda neṁba nātana piriya magaṁ
negaḻdanatanubhujavikramanē doreyara mātana bhujabaladorege
varalneṟeyarēṁ pratāpiyo muddaṁ . 4 Avaranvayatiḷakaṁ lōkaviśrutaṁ pi
riya kōṭigāvuṇḍaṁ saṁbhavisida nātana māḍida śivagṛhamuṁ jainasāle
yuṁ bhūvidita . 5 nayamaṁ meṟevaṁ tatsutanayanāyaṁ paranenisi nega
ḻda piriyāycana nāśrayaṇīya nāda nākṣatriyādigaṁ sakaḷasama
yigaṁ budhajanakaṁ . 6 caṇḍabhujavi(ṁ)kramaṁ bhūmaṇḍalavikhyāta nātaniṁ baḷikaṁ gā
vuṇḍige ninda nāhavaśauṇḍaṁ toṇḍaṁ tadanvavāya najēyaṁ . 7 piriya magaṁ kā
ḷidhuraṁdhara nātaṁ toṇḍigeṁge meccisidōṁ kannaradēvana nāḍoḍeyarppariṇata
rārkkandukakka rēvantananēṁ . 8 baḷavadvidviṭtamō ṟe mitrāṁ bujaṅga
ḷnija śrīnilayat vikasise khaḷavarggōtpaḷatējōnaḷata
ptaṁ dīnavṛddhadvijanaṭabhaṭasaccakrayugmaṁ sarugmaṁ naliyuttaṁ nōḍe tannaṁ be
ḷaguva neḷeyoḷbeḷvalādityadēvaṁ . 9 Ātma(ṁ)jñānaprasaṅgaṁ dorekoḷe besago
ṇḍaṁphaḷaṁ bhūmidānāncita ḷi darpparppaṭu mō
nāṅga tajñātāśēṣāgama rnniścita matimanuge rjjā
tōrvvīśasāṁkhyāsamasamaya maṭha vī nāgasō
mayāji pravaracaturvvēdi bhaṭṭa nubhava rakhiḷaśāstrapaṭugaḷ
dvēdigaḷ jātavēdabhaṭṭa rnnegaḻdar . Āvēdārṇnavajātarāsi taneyaṁ cāvuṇḍa
bhaṭṭaṁ vacaśrīvistāritabhūtaḷaṁ sadamaḷa(ṁ)cētaśritarvviṣṇu sa
hiṣṇudharmmanirataṁ muḷgundavāstavya nāśāviśrāntayaśaṁ budhapriya na
gastyarṣyanvavāyōdbhavaṁ . Enisida puṇyamūrttiga ḷadhiśrutakīrttiga ḷiṣṭa
dānasadguṇa mellamo ndirppareṁ bī kṣi tiyoḷa gu
ṇanidhi vēdaśāstrapariniṣṭhitavāgvibhava rkkaḷāvidāgraṇi dorekoḷvudeṁbu
didu puṇyaphaḷaṁ kṛtapuṇya[noḷ . khanidhi 902 mā
na śakāṁkada vikramābda phālguṇa da dviditadi hadoḷprabhu ka
rcci kāla nā guṇanidhi gitta narttiyo[ḷe le]yde dharmmama mānyamāge kāra
ṇaguripaṭṭiyaṁ dvija tāgrahāramaṁ . śrīmaccāḷukya
taccakriya padakamaḷāmōdapuṣṭāṅgaṁ na śōbhanaṁ beḷvalapurika
radēśādhipaṁ kūrttakāntaṁṟaḷicaṟu ghriyaṁ pūjisidanī dhanadiṁ dhanyarā
mānya ragniṣṭōmāptōryyāma rssarvvakratusavanōpētaṁ biṭṭudaṁ cakri kēḷdaṁ . Alligi
tāmeḻtandandallade santōṣamāge dēvarggendamati ballaha nuṁbaḷiyaṁ dviguṇōlla sitaṁ
garghya mentu koṭṭa nanaghadhanaṁ catramaṁ dēvaracīraṁ kanakadaṇḍaṁ bhēriyendindēvaṁ vai
rivadhovaidhavyadīkṣāguru sakaḷamahīvallabhaṁ rāgadi ndāhavamallaṁ koṭṭa neṁ
bī mahimargge doreverttirppanta geydanatyutsavadindaṁ vājapēyakratuva
nuditasatkīrtti cāvuṇḍabhaṭṭaṁ . Avarindaṁ kiṟiya rllākuḷeda siddhāntanayavē
digaḷā ravaravō liṣṭāpūrttōtsavayuktarddēvabhakta ramaḷa rssāktar . kappurada
karaṇḍagegeṇeyappa sabhāmaṁṭapakke nārāyaṇa mintappudu mukhakāntiyu
goppidudidu lalitamādudene māḍisidar . ritvigpārvvaṇe satrapūje bahu
lōkēṣṭāḷimṛṣṭāṁnnadānitvaṁ dēvasaparyyemaṁtra makhiḷaṁ saṁpūrṇna mi sōmayā
jitvaṁ tatvagamendu baṇnise janaṁ yajñakriyōpāttasaṁpatvarssa ndeṟeyamma
dīkṣitaravō lmattārddharābhāgadoḷ . Antavarmmanneyado ḷsarvvanamasyamuṁ śrīma
dāhavamalladēvaralli mūnūṟu gadyāṇaṁ dharmma Ettajgeyye pañcāra saṁvajamāgi pya
ḍedu . Ūraṁ vidvajjanakkādaradoḷ bharaṇangeydu gāmuṇḍa rsamyaktāmōda rssaṁkhyeya
mmāḍida radhipatiga ḷtānu maynūṟu mattarumaṁ tammaṁgīnūṟāytadaṟoḷagadaṟo
ḷmattarirppattanā bhūsāraṁ satrakke biṭṭa heggaḍe mahī taḷaṁ rakṣiparggōtrajātar . dē variṁge
tudiyakaṁbava suriyalmattarāṟu Idanaḷidaṁ paṁcamahāpātakaṁ ©
sāmānyōyaṁ dharmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kāḷēkāḷē pāḷanīyō bhavadbhiḥ . ssarvvānētānbhāginaṟpā
rtthivēndrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmabhadraḥ . yasyayasya yadā bhūmistasyatasyaThe continuation is missing.
Digital edition of SII 11.45 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
